posts.title: Youth Summer Retreat 2026: help shape the Friday talks
text_blocks: Greenwich Madina Trust’s six-week Youth Summer Retreat begins Monday 20 July—and young people, families and the wider community are invited to help shape its special Friday talks.

The programme runs Monday to Thursday until the final week of August, with Islamic learning, practical workshops and activities for boys and girls aged 5–16. Children do not need to be enrolled at the Madrasah to take part.
Activities include learning about the life of Prophet Muḥammad ﷺ, a practical Ṣalāh workshop, selected aḥādīth and Islamic teachings, quizzes, stories, football, a planned trip and a barbecue.

There will be no Summer Retreat or Madrasah session at the Educational Centre on Fridays. Instead, young people are expected to attend the 1pm Jumuʿah at Ghousia Masjid, where a special English talk will be delivered for them.

Because these Friday talks are intended for retreat attendees aged 5–16, GMT is asking young people to answer directly and parents, carers and community members to respond with attendees in mind. The questionnaire takes about two minutes and no name or email is needed.
Existing Madrasah students do not need to register for the retreat. Parents and carers of other children should contact Greenwich Madina Trust to book a place.

key_details.label: Dates
key_details.value: Monday 20 July to the final week of August 2026

key_details.label: Monday–Thursday
key_details.value: 5:00pm–6:30pm at GMT Educational Centre, 46 Conway Road, Plumstead, SE18 1AR

key_details.label: Fridays
key_details.value: Arrive by 1:00pm at Ghousia Masjid, 56 Camrose Street, London, SE2 0JA

key_details.label: Who can attend
key_details.value: Boys and girls aged 5–16, including children not currently enrolled at the Madrasah

key_details.label: Questionnaire
key_details.value: About two minutes; young people and adults may respond; no name or email needed

key_details.label: Registration
key_details.value: Existing Madrasah students do not need to register; all others should call +44 7792 884795

posts.title: A New Way to Support the Masjid
text_blocks: Make a secure donation in a few moments, with Gift Aid available for UK taxpayers.
Use the Donate now button below to open Greenwich Madina Trust’s secure Hibabox donation page.
Your support helps maintain the masjid, support learning, and improve community spaces for the whole community.
If you are a UK taxpayer, please select YES for Gift Aid. Gift Aid boosts your donation by 25% at no extra cost to you.
Greenwich Madina Trust is a registered UK charity, number 1129277.

key_details.label: Gift Aid
key_details.value: UK taxpayers can select YES for Gift Aid to increase their donation by 25% at no extra cost

poster.alt: Poster announcing that online donations are now live for Greenwich Madina Trust

posts.title: Ghousia Masjid – New Entrance/Exit for Jummah Salaat
text_blocks: السلام عليكم ورحمة الله وبركاته
Alhumdulillah GMT are pleased to announce that a new entrance and exit for Jummah Salaat has become usable following the clearance of the Masjid garden.
Also the Masjid can now offer an extra 40 prayer spaces, weather permitting.
Brothers are welcome to access the Masjid from the pathway between No30 and No32 Myra Street, SE2 0HA.
In Sha Allah the new entrance will help create a safer and better Jummah experience for the Community and help reduce the time and congestion when exiting the masjid.
We are continuing to develop this space and are working on making the necessary shoe storage and exploring potential for ablution area.
JazakAllah to All the volunteers who gave their time, effort and suggestions and made this all possible, May Allah Almighty bless them with the best in this world and the next, Ameen.
Please continue to share your thoughts and suggestions to improve the Masjid.
جزاک اللہ خیرا
GMT Trustees

posts.title: Shaheed Quran Collection and Correct Disposal
text_blocks: السلام عليكم ورحمة الله وبركاته
GMT in cooperation with Shaheed Quran Team have arranged for the collection and correct disposal of Shaheed Qurans and other items for a fee of £3.50 per kilo.
Please ensure you have an idea of item weight before attending the Masjid and handing over your items to a member of the Trust.
Please see the attached poster and link for further information.
https://vm.tiktok.com/ZNRVR9vT2/
جزاک اللہ خیرا۔

poster.alt: Poster for Shaheed Quran collection and disposal announcement

posts.title: A Legacy of Courage
text_blocks: Greenwich Madina Trust Ghousia Masjid presents an inspiring and spiritually uplifting evening: “A Legacy of Courage” - a special gathering in remembrance of the martyrs of Uhud and in honour of the legacy of Sayyiduna Ameer Hamzah, whose courage and sacrifice continue to inspire generations.
This blessed event also serves as an Esale Thawaab gathering for the beloved wife of Haji Mohammed Ramzan.
Join us as we come together as a community to reflect, remember, and send rewards, seeking mercy and elevation for our departed loved ones.
We are honoured to be joined by esteemed scholars and speakers who will share powerful reminders and reflections:
Shaykh Asrar Rashid
Mawlana Haroon Raza
Imam Bilal Ibn Asif
Mawlana Asad Raza Attari (Naat)
Mawlana Irfan Naqshbandi

Come with family and friends to benefit from an evening filled with remembrance, reflection, and love for those who gave everything for the sake of truth. Let us reconnect with our history, strengthen our faith, and honour a legacy that lives on in our hearts.

key_details.label: Location
key_details.value: GMT Ghousia Masjid, 56 Camrose St, London SE2 0JA

poster.alt: Poster for A Legacy of Courage event
